pattern: Since each encoded value is just the xor of two consecutive original values, and xor-ing a value with itself cancels to zero, xor-ing the known first element with each encoded value in turn peels off one original element at a time.
hints: encoded[i] equals arr[i] xor arr[i+1], and xor is its own inverse, so arr[i+1] = arr[i] xor encoded[i].
Start from the given first element and walk forward through encoded, xor-ing each entry with the most recently recovered value to get the next one.
The result always has exactly one more element than encoded, with the given first value in position zero.

edge_inputs.description: smallest possible encoded array, two-element original
every encoded value is zero, so the array is constant
encoded values recover a run that passes back through zero
encoded values at the maximum allowed magnitude

stress.description: a random original array is generated first, then encoded is derived from it so decoding is always exact
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size + 1, 9999))
    arr = [rng.randint(0, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    encoded = [arr[i] ^ arr[i + 1] for i in range(n - 1)]
    return [encoded, arr[0]]
stress.note: encoded is built directly from a random arr, so the harness's expected output is exactly that arr
